entries.name: Closeout ceremony
entries.situation: A session ends; work must be reconciled, routed, committed+pushed, and a handoff left at the resume-contract bar.
entries.solution: Run /wai-closeout (templates/commands/wai-closeout.md) — reconcile lugs, route outputs, commit+push, leave a resume-contract handoff.

entries.name: Per-turn track entry
entries.situation: Every Claude-response turn must leave one durable JSONL track entry so no turn is lost.
entries.solution: Write WAI-Spoke/runtime/track-buffer.json each turn; Stop hook flushes to track.jsonl; synthesize_turn.py is the safety-net floor.

entries.name: Test-at-birth promotion gate
entries.situation: No lug may move draft->open without a runnable verification_test covering every acceptance criterion.
entries.solution: lug-reviewer + tools/validate_lug_v4.py enforce a non-empty verification_test with AC<->test traceability.

entries.name: Inbox review first
entries.situation: Every session must read incoming/ lugs before acting on its own goal — actioning can defer, reading cannot.
entries.solution: Wakeup (/wai) step 1 scans WAI-Spoke/lugs/incoming/ and surfaces items before goal work.

entries.name: Savepoint resume contract
entries.situation: A paused session must leave a savepoint a fresh agent can resume from with zero hand-feeding (P12).
entries.solution: /wai-savepoint composes the resume contract; tools/validate_savepoint.py is the hard gate that refuses a thin savepoint.

entries.name: Manifest MD5 integrity
entries.situation: A spoke's managed/ files must be verifiable against the master MANIFEST so unauthorized edits are flagged.
entries.solution: tools/manifest_build.py --verify computes MD5 per managed file and reports mismatches vs MANIFEST.json.

entries.name: Tool Advisor cross-tool audit
entries.situation: Cross-tool config can drift; a periodic audit surfaces optimization proposals.
entries.solution: Ozi/Tool Advisor runs /wai-tool-advisor on drift or stale setup.

entries.name: Savepoint at context RED
entries.situation: When context budget hits RED, work should be preserved before compaction risk.
entries.solution: Context guard prompts a /wai-savepoint at RED so nothing is lost to compaction.

entries.name: Dev server reachable at shortname.local with no port (HMR/WebSocket included)
entries.situation: A spoke runs a web dev server (Next.js/Vite/etc.) in WSL2 and the operator wants to open it at http://shortname.local (bare :80, no port) on Windows. The naive setup (Windows hosts + netsh portproxy over WSL2 default localhostForwarding) silently DROPS the HMR WebSocket upgrade: Caddy forwards the WS and returns HTTP 101, but the WSL2 relay discards it, so HMR-dependent apps (notably Next.js + Turbopack) stall on hydration. The visible symptom is a dev script telling users 'do NOT open http://shortname.local (bare :80) ... always include the port' (e.g. keeping-open-lines / kol.local:3140). New spokes inherit this broken pattern because v4 onboarding never wires the dev proxy at all.
entries.solution:
Optimal config has FOUR layers, all required for bare :80 + working HMR: (1) REGISTER the spoke in WAI-Harness/hub/local/hub-registry.json with dev: {hostname: '.local', primary_port:

, port_block: [

, ]} (the port assigner gives a unique block). (2) CADDY: run WAI-Harness/hub/local/tools/generate_caddyfile.py to regenerate /etc/caddy/Caddyfile with `http://.local { reverse_proxy localhost:

}`; Caddy forwards WebSocket upgrades transparently (returns 101) and needs no extra WS config. (3) WSL2 NETWORKING (the piece the naive setup misses): set `[wsl2]\nnetworkingMode=mirrored` in the Windows %UserProfile%\.wslconfig, then `wsl --shutdown` to apply. Mirrored networking makes WSL services reachable from Windows WITHOUT the lossy localhostForwarding relay, so the HMR WebSocket upgrade survives. This OBSOLETES the netsh portproxy 127.0.0.1:80->WSL_IP:80 hack. (4) HOSTS: ensure `127.0.0.1 .local` is in the Windows hosts file (WAI-Harness/hub/local/scripts/sync-hosts.ps1, run as Administrator). Reference doc: WAI-Harness/hub/managed/FLEET_DEV_PROXY.md. Anti-pattern: keeping default localhostForwarding + netsh portproxy and working around it by forcing the port into the URL — that defeats the shortname alias and breaks HMR.
entries.decision_rationale: Authored at the local layer as recommended; promotion to hub-mandated + fleet distribution + v4-onboarding wiring is routed to Basher via change-basher-cap-dev-shortname-no-port-v1 so every new spoke wires this automatically instead of inheriting the port-in-URL workaround.
